title: Personalised news needs to do more than just show you what you already like.
body:
For a long time, the algorithms powering our news feeds have been stuck in a loop. They show us more of the same, hoping to keep us clicking. But that is not actually helpful. It creates a filter bubble that limits our view of the world rather than broadening it.

At Headlinne, we are mapping out a different path. We believe AI should act like a skilled human editor, not just a mirror. The next step for us is about context and clarity. It is about understanding that you might want to read about tech, but you also need to understand how global trade impacts your local economy.

True personalisation is not about guessing your biases. It is about respecting your time. It means delivering the stories that matter to you, but also introducing the perspectives you might have missed. We are building tools that help you stay informed without the noise, focusing on quality over engagement metrics.

We are excited about where this is heading because it puts the reader back in control. It is a shift from passive scrolling to active understanding.
cta: You can see how we are putting this into practice at HEADLINNE.com.
